intro: Cassia senna lozenges are a natural remedy often used to support digestive health and relieve constipation. They contain the extracts of Cassia senna, a plant-based laxative that helps to stimulate bowel movements and promote regularity. When consumed as a lozenge, the active ingredients are absorbed through the mouth, bypassing the digestive system, and providing a gentle, yet effective, laxative effect.
uses: Cassia senna lozenge is used to alleviate digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, due to its natural laxative properties. It may also help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce inflammation. Additionally, some people use cassia senna lozenge to support healthy blood sugar levels and cholesterol.
benefits: Cassia senna lozenge has health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting a restful night's sleep due to its natural laxative properties and ability to relax the body. It may also help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and support overall gut health. Additionally, cassia senna lozenge is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in fighting off infections and promoting a healthy immune system.
properties: Cassia senna lozenge has therapeutic properties such as soothing the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and providing antimicrobial benefits. It may also help alleviate symptoms associated with colds, sore throats, and coughs. Additionally, its expectorant properties can help relieve congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system.
side_effects: Cassia senna lozenge can have side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its laxative properties. It may also cause allergic reactions, such as hives or itching, in some individuals. Additionally, consuming high amounts of cassia senna lozenge can lead to dehydration, electrolyte imbalances, and dependence on the product.
recipe: To make cassia senna lozenge for medicinal use, combine 1 tablespoon of dried cassia senna leaves with 1 cup of water in a saucepan, then simmer for 10 minutes to create a strong infusion. Strain the liquid and mix it with 1 cup of honey and 1 tablespoon of glycerin to create a sweet and soothing mixture. Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before pouring it into a mold to create the lozenges.
